subsection.title: The theorem
subsection.p: This is the generalized Stokes theorem.

subsection.title: Degree matching
subsection.p: If M is k-dimensional, then \omega is a (k-1)-form and d\omega is a k-form.

table.title: Degree matching between a region, its form, and the derivative.

subsection.title: Fundamental Theorem of Calculus
subsection.p: Let M=[a,b], and let \omega=f. Then d\omega=df=f'(x)\,dx.

subsection.title: Green's theorem: circulation form
Green's theorem: flux form
Stokes' theorem in space
Divergence theorem
subsection.p:
Let
\Phi_{\mathbf F} = P\,dy\wedge dz+Q\,dz\wedge dx+R\,dx\wedge dy.

subsection.title: Orientation rules

ol.li: The form is defined on the region and its boundary.
The needed partial derivatives exist and are continuous.
The boundary has the induced orientation.
Singularities are not inside the region unless handled separately.
The surface or region is piecewise smooth.

warning.p: But closed does not always imply exact.
A missing point or hole in the domain can prevent a potential from existing globally.

subsection.title: Boundary of a boundary
subsection.p: These are two sides of the same idea.
